mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains at or near zero while inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows persistent upward momentum. This combination signals monetary accommodation lagging inflation dynamics, which erodes real returns and compresses equity valuations. Negative S&P 500 returns follow within 20 trading days as investors reprice inflation risk.
regime_hint: post-crisis zero-rate environment with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines steadily below 5.5% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains above 200k per month. This tight labor market signals wage pressure and reduced slack, prompting Fed tightening expectations that raise discount rates and depress equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.9% as of Oct 2015 suggests labor slack remains
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with Fed normalization expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite tight labor conditions, or correlation between UNRATE decline and forward returns becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) exhibit a sustained 3-month declining trend while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600k annualized units. This dual contraction in consumer and residential demand signals weakening aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days as earnings expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 366404M and HOUST at 626k as of Sep 2015
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized weakness in consumption and housing
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between (RSAFS + HOUST) composite and forward returns < 0.05 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while remaining below 102.5 index level. This supply-side weakness signals deteriorating manufacturing capacity utilization and reduced profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings quality downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day returns remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) remains above 2.0% annualized while housing starts (HOUST) sustain above 620k units. This combination signals robust underlying demand momentum and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13254.7B and HOUST at 626k as of Jul 2015
regime_hint: sustained expansion with positive demand momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of non-triggered periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate remains below 0.5% while core inflation (proxied by CPIAUCSL) stays below 2.0% year-over-year. This accommodative stance with subdued inflation permits continued equity multiple expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.2% and CPIAUCSL at 217.73 as of Oct 2015
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between accommodative monetary conditions and forward returns < 0.04 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) grows at an average rate below 150k per month over a 3-month rolling window. This sub-trend labor market growth signals weakening economic momentum and reduced consumer income growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns within 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between PAYEMS growth rate and forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits sustained month-over-month growth above 0.3% while unemployment (UNRATE) declines below 5.5%. This combination signals robust supply-side capacity expansion coupled with tight labor utilization, supporting margin expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month average INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND UNRATE < 5.5%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.27 and UNRATE at 9.9% as of Oct 2015
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between (INDPRO growth + UNRATE decline) composite and forward returns < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.
